name: Affinity Relationship Intelligence CRM API
what_it_does: Relationship intelligence CRM API that maps network connections, interaction history, and relationship strength across people, organizations, and opportunities.
use_cases: Querying relationship strength scores to prioritize warm introductions for agent-driven outreach
Enriching opportunity records with network path data to identify best introduction routes
Logging meeting and email interaction data to keep relationship timeline current
Building deal-flow automation that routes new opportunities based on team relationship scores
Extracting organization and person relationship graphs for network analysis in agent pipelines
not_for: Traditional sales CRM workflows where call logging, quoting, and pipeline forecasting are primary needs
High-volume transactional contact management at scale (Salesforce or HubSpot better)
Teams not in relationship-intensive industries like VC, PE, investment banking, or recruiting
best_when: An agent needs to leverage network relationship data and warm path mapping to prioritize outreach or route opportunities in deal-flow or BD contexts.
avoid_when: Your use case is standard sales pipeline management without a need for relationship graph intelligence or network path analysis.

auth.notes: API key passed via HTTP Basic auth (empty username, key as password). Account-level key with no scope granularity. Keys managed from admin settings.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; demo available on request
pricing.paid_tiers: Custom enterprise pricing; typically $500–$2000+/user/year depending on team size and features
pricing.notes: Enterprise-tier pricing with annual contracts. Not self-serve — requires sales engagement. API access included on all plans.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors return HTTP codes with JSON messages; field-level validation errors are present but can lack remediation detail.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Agents should check for existing entities before creating to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: Relationship strength scores are computed asynchronously from email/calendar data; agents reading scores immediately after data ingestion may see stale values
The 'path' (introduction route) feature requires the connected Google/Outlook account to have sufficient interaction data — cold accounts return empty paths
No official SDK; agents must build REST client from scratch against a relatively small but well-documented API surface
List fields (custom field arrays) use a different API structure than scalar fields; agents handling dynamic field types need to inspect field metadata first
Annual enterprise contracts required — no self-serve API access means agent prototypes cannot be tested without a sales process

security_score_components.security_notes: Single API key with no scope restrictions. Key grants full read/write access. HTTPS enforced. No OAuth option currently. SOC2 Type II certified.
